what_score_is: The large percentage is a bounded model output from the current state vector, not a literal real-world strike frequency.
Uncertainty is model uncertainty from signal quality/conflict/corroboration depth, not a confidence interval over geopolitical reality.
Values are comparable across time under the same scoring rules and historical replay assumptions.
how_model_works: Pipeline: ingest -> normalize -> classify -> event-cluster -> bounded state update -> bounded logistic mapping.
Classification uses actor pairing, signal class detection, contradiction adjustment, and recency decay.
Event clusters group by actor pair, class, time bucket, and normalized title to reduce duplicate-wire inflation.
Class hierarchy down-weights commentary/rhetoric relative to operational actions.
why_drivers_shown: Drivers are ranked by modeled contribution magnitude after clustering, class weighting, corroboration, and decay.
Displayed rows include source domain, class, impact, confidence, and corroboration count for inspection.
Node-click filtering switches drivers to the nearest diagnostics slice for that selected historical point.
history_policy: History is replay-based and scored through the same model path as current data.
Stored series include event/scoring timestamps and diagnostics rows with driver payloads.
Coverage quality depends on external archive/API completeness and may vary by date range.
limitations: Token/time clustering can still miss some semantically equivalent events.
Backtesting is lightweight and currently focused on direction/pair sanity checks.
Model confidence should not be read as ground-truth certainty.
worked_examples: Single-source missile test: medium movement with elevated uncertainty.
Multi-source corroborated strike event: stronger confidence, bounded impact uplift.
Speech/commentary flood without operations: clustered and heavily down-weighted.
Ceasefire claim without verification terms: weak stabilization effect.
